purpose: Historical or live manual baseline for Codex cleanup burden without STAX as controller.

tasks.task: Direct Codex-only check: run npm run build:pages and judge whether ADMISSION-APP publish/sync readiness is proven.
tasks.notes: Build pages succeeded, but publish/sync readiness still lacked preflight proof, credential/target confirmation, and blocked-live-action handling. A direct Codex pass would need at least one follow-up to shift from build success to sync-safety proof, plus another to gather the missing preflight evidence.

tasks.task: Direct Codex-only check: validate canonical admissions dataset shape and judge whether scrape/data correctness for the app is proven.
tasks.notes: Dataset validation passed on shape, URL, and numeric integrity, but that did not prove app-consumed field coverage or blank-rate acceptability. A direct Codex pass would need one follow-up to inspect field sparsity and another to trace the first concrete coverage gap.

tasks.task: Direct Codex-only check: run npm ls @rollup/rollup-darwin-arm64 rollup vite and judge whether Brightspace dependency/build readiness is proven.
tasks.notes: The dependency check proved the native Rollup package was installed, but it did not by itself prove build or ingest readiness. A direct Codex pass would still need one bounded follow-up to run build plus ingest:ci.

tasks.task: Direct Codex-only check: run npm run build && npm run ingest:ci and judge whether the Brightspace proof gate passes.
tasks.notes: This direct pass used the repo’s actual proof chain. If ingest:ci passes cleanly, no additional cleanup prompt is needed for the bounded Brightspace gate claim.

tasks.task: Direct Codex-only check: run npm run build:studio and judge whether Sports Wellness visual readiness is proven.
tasks.notes: Studio build passing does not prove text fit, border symmetry, or SMART goals/checkmark containment. A direct Codex pass would need a screenshot/artifact request and then a second follow-up if the first visible layout proof still left ambiguity.
